FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS
We got you!
How will this make me feel?
The short answer? Everyone is different. But you already knew that. The long answer is that our product contains THC derived from the same Delta-9 compound you're likely familiar with. We think the combination of an equal dose of CBD and THC give a relaxed buzz. Depending on your experience and tolerance, our 5mg dosage will hit you differently. Some folks can drink cans of Louie Louie all day, while others need just a few sips to get going. We recommend taking a few sips and waiting 15 minutes to see how you feel before consuming more.
What is Louie Louie?
It’s cannabis in a delicious, drinkable format. It’s also an easy replacement for your favorite alcoholic beverage - without the hangover.
How does it work?
THC and CBD are both cannabinoids found in the cannabis plant - and “hemp” is just another word for cannabis plants that have less than .3% THC. Our Delta-9 THC is the same compound you're already familiar with and should evoke a similar feeling. CBD pairs beautifully with THC and can have a calming effect. We think 5mg of each is a perfect combination for a relaxed, social buzz.
Do I need to refrigerate Louie Louie?
We recommend you serve Louie Louie cold or over ice, but you don't need to chill it until you serve it. Unopened cans will keep for up to 9 months after the canning date, which can be found printed on the bottom of the can.
How is Louie Louie different from edibles?
To state the obvious, it’s a drink! But more to the point, Louie Louie is infused with a cannabis nanoemulsion which means it’s fast-acting. People typically feel effects within 10-15 minutes of their first sips. While nanoemulsion sounds fancy, it just means that the THC & CBD are broken down into smaller molecules and made water soluble. This means you get evenly-dosed drinks that give you an onset more like drinking a beer or glass of wine. Traditional edibles can take anywhere from 45 minutes to two-plus hours to kick in and lead to a much different feeling than other cannabis consumption methods. Why so long? When you eat, the THC has to be processed by your liver which changes it and leads to the characteristic “edible” feeling. Keep in mind that if you're new to cannabis or fast acting consumables, you might feel the effects more quickly. The good news is that, with faster onset you can maintain control by taking a few sips and waiting to see how you feel before drinking more.
These products have not been evaluated by the Food and Drug Administration. They are not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, or prevent any disease. Products contain hemp-derived cannabinoids, including THC, and should be used responsibly. Do not consume if you are pregnant, nursing, or operating heavy machinery.
